title: Switch Exhaustive
description: Use the never type to ensure your switch statements handle all possible cases of a union type.
problemStatement:
No cases missed! Create an enum `Direction` with `Up` and `Down`. Write a switch statement for a direction variable. Assign a variable of type `never` in the default case.

**Requirement:** This ensures that if you add a new direction later, TypeScript will error because the switch isn't exhaustive anymore.
hints: The `never` type is used for values that should never exist.
expectedOutput: A switch statement that perfectly handles all enum variants
